prompt: Top-down flat-lay tight detail of a paper wall calendar pinned open on a workbench. The calendar grid is visible but month and day labels are intentionally blurred and unreadable; instead, the focus is on hand-drawn red X marks crossing off a clear progression of consecutive day boxes, suggesting visible time passing. A small white plastic tile-spacer sits in the corner of the calendar grid. A few flecks of dried thinset mortar on the bench surface. A pencil stub diagonal across one corner. Shot with a 100mm macro lens directly overhead, slightly cool morning window light from the left, hard shadows from the spacer. Slightly desaturated palette except the saturated red X marks. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text — calendar labels are intentionally blurred to abstract grid only.
